_comment: Machine-readable build spec for build_form.py. Source of truth for the Chapter 1 Test Google Form quiz. Human-readable transcription: classes/foundations/_source-text/portal/ch01/test.md. Keep in sync.
title: Chapter 1 Test — Foundations (Bible 9)
description: Chapter 1 covers what a worldview is, what it means (and doesn't mean) to be a Christian, why Christians trust the Bible, and the Bible's story in six acts. Total: 87 points.

sections.heading: Matching
sections.instructions: Match each item to the correct name. 1 point each.

questions.text: 1. Act 1
2. Act 2
3. Act 3
4. Interlude
5. Act 4
6. Act 5
7. Act 6

sections.heading: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: Select ALL correct answers. Some questions have more than one. (Auto-scored as all-or-nothing; the teacher awards partial credit in the Forms grading view where needed.)

questions.text: 8. Which of the following are worldviews?
9. What conditions do you have to meet to become a Christian?
options: You have to eat meat and drink alcohol.
You have to be born into a Christian family.
You have to obey every command in Scripture.
You have to go to church.

questions.text: 10. Which of the following describe Jesus?
options: He was a teacher and a healer.
correct: He was a teacher and a healer.

questions.text: 11. Why do Christians care about the Bible?
options: because it is an ancient book
because God reveals himself through it
because it is a sacred object we should worship
because it tells us how to live to please God
because it is a collection of 66 books
correct: because God reveals himself through it
because it tells us how to live to please God

questions.text: 12. Which of the following happen in the grand story of the Bible?
options: Abraham comes to judge the earth.
The nation of Israel waits for a savior.
God tells people to destroy the earth.
correct: The nation of Israel waits for a savior.

questions.text: 13. Which of the following are spiritual disciplines?
14. What is a Christian's primary calling?
15. Which of the following describe Christian love?
options: loving the world as much as you love your family
accepting anything a person does as right and good for them
something that only happens between you and God

sections.heading: True / False
sections.instructions: Indicate whether each statement is true or false. 1 point each.

questions.text: 16. ALL worldviews accurately explain the world and help us find direction and purpose for our lives.
17. Being a Christian MEANS being judgmental and never having fun.
18. All Christians should be concerned about earthly problems like poverty and injustice.
19. People who are not Christians can be devout followers of another religion.
20. You ARE automatically a Christian if Christianity is part of your cultural heritage.
21. Jesus' followers were first called Christians in Antioch during the first century.
22. The word Christ IS Jesus' surname.
23. Messiah is one of the titles of Jesus.

sections.heading: Fill in the Blank
sections.instructions: Type the missing word. 2 points each. (Spelling matters; the teacher can accept variants in the grading view.)

questions.text: 24. For Christians, the ______ plays a central role in informing our thoughts, values, decisions, and actions.
25. Christians place themselves under the Bible's ______ because they believe it is God's Word.
26. Each book of the Bible is divided into chapters and then into portions called ______.
27. C. S. Lewis argued that it is a ______ to assume that we today are necessarily smarter or more enlightened than our ancestors.
28. The Tree of ______ of Good and Evil is found in the account of the fall, which is the second act of the biblical story.
29. The nation of Israel comes from the descendants of ______ family.
30. The church was formed by people who listened to Jesus' ______, who proclaimed the good news of God's kingdom.
31. Anthropology is the study of people and ______ is the study of God.
32. Spiritual ______ help us to practice and grow in our faith.
33. Because the kingdom of God has begun but is not fully realized, the church lives in a time described as ______ and not yet.

sections.heading: Short Answer
sections.instructions: Answer in complete sentences. 3 points each. (Graded by the teacher.)

questions.text: 34. What is a worldview? Explain how it can be compared to a map.
questions.modelAnswer: A worldview is a pattern of ideas and beliefs that helps us make sense of the world and guides our decisions and actions. Like a map, it is only useful if it corresponds with reality.

questions.text: 35. What happens in the first act of the grand story of the Bible?
questions.modelAnswer: God creates the universe and all that is in it, including human beings, and tells them to fill and rule the earth.

questions.text: 36. What happens in the third act of the grand story of the Bible?
questions.modelAnswer: God chooses Abraham, through whom he will bless the earth; some of Abraham's descendants become the nation of Israel.

questions.text: 37. What were God's people hoping for during the interlude?
questions.modelAnswer: More revelation from God (after ~400 years of silence) and a savior who would defeat their conquerors.

questions.text: 38. What happens in the fourth act of the grand story of the Bible?
questions.modelAnswer: The story of Jesus' life, death, and resurrection.

questions.text: 39. What happens in the last act of the grand story of the Bible?
questions.modelAnswer: Jesus returns to judge everyone; sin, death, and evil are destroyed; God makes a new heaven and earth and reigns forever.

sections.heading: Essay
sections.instructions: Answer each with at least one paragraph. 5 points each. (Graded by the teacher.)

questions.text: 40. Define the term Christian and describe three common misconceptions about what it means to be a Christian.
questions.modelAnswer: A Christian is someone who follows Jesus. Three misconceptions: that it means following lots of rules; that you must be born into a Christian family or country; that it is only about heaven.

questions.text: 41. Summarize what Christians believe about Jesus, according to your textbook.
questions.modelAnswer: Jesus is God (part of the Trinity) who became human and lived a sinless life; he taught and healed, died for our sins, rose again, returned to heaven, and will come back to judge everyone. He is the Messiah; we must trust him for forgiveness, and his followers seek to imitate him.

questions.text: 42. Describe what the main characters of the fifth act of the grand story of the Bible are supposed to do and how long it will take to do it.
questions.modelAnswer: The main characters are Jesus' followers, the church. They are to love God and others, imitate Christ, and proclaim God's kingdom — for their whole lives, until Jesus returns.
